title: calystegia collina subsp. tridactylosa
intro: Calystegia collina subsp. tridactylosa, commonly known as the Three-fingered Morning Glory, is a plant species within the Convolvulaceae family. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties and is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. The therapeutic actions of Calystegia collina subsp. tridactylosa include relaxing muscles, reducing pain and inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents present in this plant include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties.
benefits: Calystegia collina subsp. tridactylosa has many benefits, such as its extracts being used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels, prevent cancer, and improve cardiovascular health. Additionally, this plant is known to have antiviral, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it effective in treating infections, wounds, and skin conditions like acne and eczema. The benefits of Calystegia collina subsp. tridactylosa also extend to improving cognitive function, reducing stress and anxiety, and promoting better sleep quality, ultimately leading to a more balanced and healthy lifestyle. By harnessing the power of this plant, individuals can experience improved overall well-being, increased energy levels, and a greater sense of vitality, enabling them to live a more fulfilling and productive life.
constituents: Calystegia collina subsp. tridactylosa has many active constituents, such as alkaloids, flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which possess medicinal properties that can improve people's daily lives. These constituents, including calystegines, coniine, and solanocapsine, have been found to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, which can help protect against chronic diseases like cancer, diabetes, and cardiovascular disease. For example, the flavonoids present in this plant have been shown to have cardio-protective effects, improve cognitive function, and even exhibit anti-cancer properties, making it a valuable resource for promoting overall health and well-being. Furthermore, the saponins found in Calystegia collina subsp. tridactylosa have been found to have immunomodulatory effects, which can help boost the immune system and prevent illnesses like the common cold and flu.
parts: Calystegia collina subsp. tridactylosa has many medicinal parts, such as the roots, leaves, and stems, which contain various constituents with potential therapeutic properties. The roots of this plant are rich in alkaloids, including scopoletin and umbelliferone, which have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The leaves and stems contain glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, including quercetin and kaempferol, which may possess antimicrobial and antitumor properties. Additionally, the plant's flowers have been found to contain anthocyanins and carotenoids, which have been linked to antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
side_effects: Calystegia collina subsp. tridactylosa can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing vomiting, diarrhea, and stomach pain due to its high levels of toxic compounds. Ingesting large quantities of the plant's leaves or stems may also lead to more severe side effects, including tremors, seizures, and even respiratory failure. Additionally, exposure to the plant's sap can cause skin irritation, including redness, itching, and blistering in some individuals. Prolonged or repeated exposure to the plant's toxic compounds may also increase the risk of liver and kidney damage.
